commands.repair_reason: - [P2] This protected maintainer PR needs human review on profile semantics, runtime provisioning, workflow provenance, and proof boundaries rather than cleanup closure or an automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff adds a workflow path that clones, installs, builds, and executes an external runtime outside the repository dependency graph.; Review finding: [P1] Provide Crabline before selecting smoke-ci — e... (sha=7f0906dd495c502815cf8e11866c579eea4fee5d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Crabline-backed QA Lab channel driver, channel-driver CLI/profile routing, live-profile evidence merging, Matrix evidence output changes, docs, workflow coverage, and tests.

PR surface: Source +1450, Tests +764, Docs +19, Config +68, Other +2. Total +2303 across 28 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection is enough to reproduce the review blockers: smoke-ci selects Crabline, selected channel scenarios call generic actions, and the new Crabline transport throws for those actions. This read-only review did not run the workflow.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This protected maintainer PR needs human review on profile semantics, runtime provisioning, workflow provenance, and proof boundaries rather than cleanup closure or an automated repair lane.

target.title: feat(qa): crabline channel driver
target.body:
## Summary

This PR makes QA profile channel drivers executable and separates channel identity from transport mode.

- Runs the `smoke-ci` QA profile through Crabline local mocks, defaulting to the Telegram channel shape without requiring Telegram credentials or Convex leases.
- Adds a Crabline-backed QA transport that maps requested channel IDs such as `telegram` or `slack` onto local mock providers and records native QA evidence through the QA Lab bus.
- Keeps live channel execution on the native live transports, where unsupported requested channels fail as missing coverage instead of silently falling back.
- Makes the `release` live profile fan out to the native Matrix, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp live lane catalogs, then merges their `qa-evidence.json` outputs into one profile evidence artifact.
- Converts Matrix live QA to emit native QA evidence while preserving its Matrix-specific diagnostic summary.

## Why

`smoke-ci` should be a deterministic CI profile for channel-shaped behavior, not a secret-bearing live service run. Live service coverage belongs in the live transport lanes and release profile evidence. Keeping those concepts separate lets tests ask for `channel: telegram` while the selected profile decides whether that Telegram-shaped exchange is mocked locally or executed against the real Telegram transport.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.extensions.json`
- `pnpm vitest run extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts`
target.author: romneyda

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
